title: University of the Free State (UFS) 2027 Applications Now Open: Complete Step-by-Step Guide for Undergraduate, Postgraduate, and International Applicants
summary: The University of the Free State (UFS) has officially opened applications for the 2027 academic year , providing prospective students with an opportunity to access quality higher education in South Africa. Applications are free of charge and conducted entirely online through the UFS application portal. Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early, particularly for highly competitive programmes with earlier closing dates. This detailed guide provides accurate and complete information on the application process, deadlines, requirements, document submission, and official support contacts. Application Opening Date Applications opened on: Wednesday, 1 April 2026 Application fee: Free Step-by-Step UFS Online Application Process The UFS online application system consists of eight mandatory steps : Start Application Selection Information Study Choices Verification Personal Information Uploads Agreement Completion All steps must be completed for the application to be successfully submitted. After submission: You will receive a confirmation email You will be issued a student number You can track your application via the Self-Service portal Types of Applications Available 1. Undergraduate Applications Open to Grade 12 learners and high school graduates Applicants may select up to two study programmes Must meet minimum admission requirements 2. Postgraduate Applications Includes Honours, Postgraduate Diplomas, Master’s, and PhD programmes Requires academic transcripts and supporting documents 3. Specialized Schools Business School School of Financial Planning Law Applicants must consult the relevant faculty or prospectus for detailed programme requirements. Click HERE to apply for the Scholarship. Important Application Deadlines (2027 Intake) Undergraduate Deadlines 31 May 2026 MB ChB (Medicine) Occupational Therapy Optometry Physiotherapy Radiation Science Biokinetics Dietetics Sports Coaching 31 July 2026 Nursing Social Work Architecture 31 August 2026 All international undergraduate applications 30 September 2026 Transfer students from other institutions All remaining undergraduate programmes (selection non-selection) Applicants should apply well before deadlines , especially for health-related programmes. International Applicants: Requirements International students must meet the following requirements: Obtain a valid study visa for South Africa (endorsed for UFS) Provide a Universities South Africa (USAf) Certificate of Exemption if they do not hold a South African National Senior Certificate Submit certified academic documents Submit predicted or mid-year results (for Cambridge or equivalent systems) International postgraduate applicants must also provide: SAQA (South African Qualifications Authority) certificate Required Documents for Application For Current Grade 12 Learners Final Grade 11 results OR June Grade 12 results For Completed Grade 12 Applicants Final Grade 12 certificate For Postgraduate Applicants Academic transcripts Degree certificates Supporting documents Important Notes Maximum file size: 5 MB per document Documents should be correctly uploaded to avoid delays Missing or incorrect documents will delay processing Submitting Results and Documents If applying with Grade 11 results, Grade 12 results must later be uploaded via the Self-Service portal Ensure all documents are accurate and complete Uploading incorrect information may delay your application Monitoring Your Application Status After submission: You will receive an acknowledgement email with your student number Updates will be sent via email You can check your status on: https://myapplication.ufs.ac.za/ Important: Application status will not be shared via phone calls Applicants must monitor their email regularly Official Support Contacts (Emails Help Channels) Undergraduate Application Support Email: applications@ufs.ac.za WhatsApp: KovsieChat – 087 240 6370 Phone: +27 (0)51 401 3000 Postgraduate Diploma Honours Support Email: studentadmin@ufs.ac.za Phone: +27 (0)51 401 9111 (Option 1) Master’s and Doctoral Applications Email: UFSPostgradAdmin@ufs.ac.za International Applicants (Documents Results) Email: oiastudentadmin@ufs.ac.za Important Application Guidelines Only apply for programmes where you meet all requirements Ensure all uploaded documents are complete and accurate Keep your contact details updated Regularly check your email for updates Apply early for competitive programmes Data Privacy and Compliance The University of the Free State processes personal data in accordance with the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA) . Applicants’ data is securely stored and used for academic and administrative purposes. Applicants are responsible for ensuring that all submitted information is accurate and up to date . Click HERE to learn more about the University of the Free State (UFS) 2027 Applications. Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY. title: Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Researchers from the Global South
summary: The Danish Institute for Human Rights has launched its Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026 , a fully funded initiative designed to strengthen international research collaboration with academics primarily based in the Global South and other underrepresented regions. This prestigious programme offers selected researchers the opportunity to work with one of Europe’s leading human rights institutions while contributing to high-impact global research. If you are a scholar passionate about human rights, governance, justice systems, or digital transformation, this is an exceptional opportunity to gain international exposure, expand your academic network, and produce meaningful research outputs. What is the Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme? The Danish Institute for Human Rights is Denmark’s National Human Rights Institution and equality body. It works nationally and globally to promote and protect human rights through research, policy advice, and strategic partnerships. Through this new visiting researcher programme, the Institute seeks to develop new and strengthen existing collaborations with researchers from underrepresented regions. The programme emphasizes equitable partnerships, mutual learning, and knowledge exchange. Key Research Themes for 2026 Successful applicants will work within one of three major thematic areas: 1. Technology and Human Rights This theme focuses on how emerging and existing technologies impact human rights and democratic governance in the Global South. Possible research topics include: Digital rights and freedoms AI governance and accountability Human rights impact assessments of technology Inclusive digital regulations and policy frameworks Rights-respecting digital transitions through development cooperation 2. National Human Rights Institutions and Preventing Violent Extremism This area examines how National Human Rights Institutions (NHRIs) can contribute to peace, security, and the prevention of violent extremism in Sahelian and coastal West African contexts. Possible research topics include: Security governance and human rights Radicalization prevention strategies Human rights-based approaches to peacebuilding Policy recommendations for regional frameworks Population protection strategies 3. Customary Justice Systems and Human Rights Standards This research theme explores how customary law, informal justice systems, community paralegals, and formal justice institutions can better protect marginalized communities. Priority groups include: Women Children People with disabilities Applicants in this area should draw on case studies from at least two of the following countries: Ethiopia Kenya Rwanda Uganda Zambia Programme Duration Affiliation with the Institute for up to 6 months Minimum 2-week visit to Copenhagen required Hybrid participation models are welcome Benefits of the Programme Selected researchers will receive substantial support, including: Research stipend / honorarium Travel costs to and from Copenhagen Visa support and costs Accommodation in Copenhagen Academic mentorship and institutional support Access to policy and international research networks Opportunity to publish policy briefs, working papers, or academic articles Required Documents Applicants must submit: Current CV (maximum 3 pages) One relevant publication in English or French Project proposal (1–5 pages) including aims, methodology, outputs, and relevance Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Human rights expertise and experience Affiliation with an academic institution in the Global South Quality of previous publications Feasibility of project proposal Relevance to the thematic focus areas Why You Should Apply This programme is more than a research visit. It is a platform for global collaboration, policy influence, and academic growth. Participants will engage with leading experts, contribute to real-world human rights solutions, and build long-term partnerships for future funding opportunities. Application Deadline 1 June 2026 How to Apply APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more internship opportunities, click here. Final Thoughts The Danish Institute for Human Rights Global Visiting Researcher Programme 2026 is one of the best fully funded research opportunities for scholars from the Global South. If your work intersects with justice, technology, governance, or human rights, this programme could be a major step forward in your academic career. Apply early and prepare a strong proposal that clearly demonstrates relevance, impact, and feasibility. title: CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany (Fully Funded): Monthly €3,000 Stipend, Travel Support & Global Eligibility
summary: The CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Berlin, Germany is now open for applications. This prestigious fully funded fellowship is designed for researchers, policy experts, think tank professionals, and practitioners working on Turkey-related issues. Hosted by the Centre for Applied Turkey Studies (CATS) at the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP) , the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to conduct impactful research in one of Europe’s leading academic and policy environments. Applicants from all countries can apply, and there is no application fee . The fellowship runs for 6 to 12 months , beginning from September 2026 , and selected fellows will be based in Berlin, Germany. Why Apply for the CATS Fellowship? This program is ideal for scholars and professionals seeking to deepen their expertise in Turkish politics, security, climate policy, digital transformation, and geopolitical affairs while collaborating with leading researchers in Germany. Selected fellows will gain access to a world-class research network, publication opportunities, and financial support to focus fully on their projects. Fellowship Benefits The CATS Fellowship is fully funded and includes: Monthly grant of €3,000 Travel allowance to and from Berlin Budget for research trips Tuition support Participation in field trips Additional family support for fellows accompanied by children Collaboration opportunities with CATS and SWP researchers Possibility of publishing research in peer-reviewed CATS and SWP outlets Research Topics for 2026/2027 Applicants are invited to submit proposals under the following themes: 1. Digitalisation and Artificial Intelligence Research may explore Turkey’s AI strategy, digital governance, cyber resilience, defense modernization, labor markets, and economic transformation through emerging technologies. 2. Critical Raw Materials (CRMs) Topics include Turkey’s rare earth minerals strategy, extraction policies, geopolitical competition, industrial upgrading, and EU-Turkey cooperation on critical resources. 3. Energy Transition and Climate Change Applicants may focus on Turkey’s climate commitments, water scarcity, renewable energy strategy, governance reforms, and environmental security. 4. Turkish Foreign and Security Policy Amid Geopolitical Turmoil Areas include NATO dynamics, Black Sea security, South Caucasus relations, regional power competition, maritime strategy, and energy corridors. Who Should Apply? This fellowship is suitable for: Postdoctoral researchers Academics and scholars Think tank analysts Policy experts Practitioners in relevant sectors Researchers with strong publication records PhD candidates with proven expertise in Turkey-related research Required Application Documents Applicants must submit one single PDF file containing: Cover letter Research proposal (maximum 5 pages) Research objectives and relevance State of research overview Methodology Bibliography CV with list of publications Academic transcripts and/or diplomas Applications may be submitted in English or German . Selection Criteria Applications will be assessed based on: Academic and professional merit Relevance to fellowship themes Feasibility and coherence of proposal Public outreach value of the project Preference may be given to applicants who have not previously received CATS funding in cases of equal merit. Deadline The application deadline is 30 April 2026 . Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply Send your complete application as a single PDF document via email to: CATSapplication@swp-berlin.org For inquiries: CATS@swp-berlin.org APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Final Thoughts If you are a researcher or policy professional looking for a fully funded fellowship in Europe, the CATS Fellowships 2026/2027 in Germany is an outstanding opportunity. With generous funding, access to influential networks, and a chance to contribute to high-level policy research, this fellowship can significantly advance your academic and professional career. title: Become a Mentor in Europe’s Leading Women Innovation Programme: Apply for the EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026
summary: The European Innovation Council (EIC) has officially opened applications for mentors to join the prestigious EIC Women Leadership Programme 2026 . This is a unique opportunity for experienced leaders across Europe’s innovation ecosystem to guide and empower the next generation of women innovators, researchers, and entrepreneurs. Running from May to November 2026 , the programme connects accomplished mentors with high-potential women building breakthrough technologies and ambitious ventures. If you are passionate about leadership, innovation, and creating impact, this mentor call offers a chance to shape the future of European entrepreneurship. What is the EIC Women Leadership Programme? The EIC Women Leadership Programme was created to support women researchers and entrepreneurs by strengthening leadership skills, expanding networks, and increasing visibility within Europe’s innovation ecosystem. Since its pilot launch in 2021, the initiative has supported more than 300 women innovators through business coaching, personal mentoring, leadership training, and networking opportunities. A core feature of the programme is its structured mentoring scheme, where experienced professionals help participants grow as leaders and scale their ventures. Why Become a Mentor? Mentors contribute on a pro bono basis while gaining access to one of Europe’s most valuable innovation networks. Key Benefits for Mentors Official mentor recognition and certification Increased visibility through EIC programme channels Inclusion in the mentors’ brochure Access to an exclusive network of investors, founders, and innovation leaders Speaking and thought leadership opportunities Exposure to cutting-edge startups and emerging technologies Opportunity to shape women’s leadership in Europe Possibility to attend the EIC Summit Invitations to networking events within the EIC ecosystem What Does a Mentor Do? Mentors support selected participants by sharing expertise, strategic guidance, and professional networks. The mentoring relationship focuses on: Leadership development and confident decision-making Strategic thinking in leadership roles Career growth and executive positioning Expanding networks and increasing visibility Scaling ventures and overcoming business challenges Mentors are carefully matched with mentees based on expertise, experience, and participant needs. Mentor Journey and Timeline The programme is designed to be efficient, structured, and respectful of mentors’ schedules. Important Dates 13 May 2026: Online onboarding workshop 30 June 2026: Mentor-mentee matching confirmed July – November 2026: Mentoring sessions and networking activities 27 November 2026: Final Programme Event, Demo Day, and Pitching Session Time Commitment Mentors will participate in: 5–6 online mentoring sessions Approximately 1 hour per session Flexible scheduling based on availability Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for professionals who want to create meaningful impact in Europe’s innovation ecosystem, including: Startup founders Entrepreneurs Angel investors Venture capital partners Senior executives Innovation consultants Ecosystem leaders How to Apply Interested applicants can submit their application through the official application form provided by the European Innovation Council. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Application Deadline 30 April 2026 (End of Business Day) Early applications are strongly recommended. Why This Opportunity Matters Women innovators continue to transform industries across Europe. By becoming a mentor in the EIC Women Leadership Programme, you can directly contribute to a more inclusive and competitive innovation landscape while expanding your own influence and network. This is more than mentorship—it is a chance to help build the future of European leadership and entrepreneurship. title: Top Free UN Online Courses 2026: Advance Your Career in Climate, Health, Sustainability & Global Development (With Certificates)
summary: In today’s rapidly evolving world, gaining relevant, future-focused skills is no longer optional—it is essential. The United Nations, through platforms such as UN SDG: Learn, UNITAR, UNICEF, UNESCO, and the World Bank Group, is offering a wide range of free, self-paced online courses designed to equip learners with practical knowledge and globally relevant competencies. These courses cover critical areas such as climate action, global health, gender equality, sustainable lifestyles, mental health, and development financing. They are accessible to learners worldwide, require flexible time commitments, and many provide certificates upon completion. Whether you are a student, professional, policymaker, or development practitioner, these programs provide an opportunity to build skills aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Comprehensive List of Free UN Online Courses (2026) 1. Sports for Climate Action (Advanced Course) This advanced-level course explores how the sports sector can contribute to combating climate change through sustainability strategies and emission reduction efforts. Key Details Level: Advanced Duration: 4 hours Format: Self-paced Certificate: Yes Language: English, Spanish What You Will Learn Climate mitigation and adaptation strategies in sports Sustainable supply chains in sports organizations Stakeholder engagement for climate action Monitoring and reporting sustainability progress Course Modules Winning the Climate Game The Game Plan for Green Sports Sustainable Supply Chains Engagement and Impact Tracking Progress Learn More 2. Sustainable Lifestyles (Introductory Course) This course focuses on how individual and collective lifestyle choices impact sustainability and how to transition toward responsible consumption patterns. Key Details Level: Introductory Duration: 3 hours Certificate: Yes Core Learning Areas Understanding sustainable consumption patterns Lifestyle impacts on the environment Policy frameworks supporting sustainability Practical solutions tailored to different regions Learn More 3. Adolescent Girls’ Agency, Safety and Wellbeing This learning pathway focuses on gender-transformative approaches and improving outcomes for adolescent girls globally. Key Details Level: Introductory Duration: 2.5 hours Certificate: Yes Learning Outcomes Gender-transformative programming strategies Ending child marriage and FGM Socio-ecological model application Empowerment and inclusion of vulnerable girls Learn More 4. One Health in Practice: Ecosystems Human Health A comprehensive course exploring the link between biodiversity, ecosystems, and human health. Key Details Level: Intermediate Duration: 10 weeks Effort: 2–3 hours/week Certificate: No Key Topics Zoonotic diseases and pandemics Environmental determinants of health Ecosystem resilience frameworks Governance for health and biodiversity Learn More 5. Mental Health and Psychosocial Support A microlearning course focused on mental health support in conflict and post-conflict settings. Key Details Duration: 1 hour Certificate: No What You Will Gain Understanding psychosocial wellbeing Tools for community support Conflict-sensitive mental health strategies Learn More 6. Global Public Health This course provides a deep dive into global health systems and their connection to development. Key Details Duration: 10 weeks Effort: 4–6 hours/week Certificate: No Learning Focus Global health systems and infrastructure Impact of environment, migration, and trade Health as a driver of development Learn More 7. Measles Control in Emergencies A specialized course focusing on outbreak prevention and response in humanitarian contexts. Key Details Duration: 2 hours Certificate: Yes Key Learning Areas Risk assessment in emergencies Outbreak investigation Immunization strategies Learn More 8. Communication for Immunization A practical course on designing communication strategies to improve vaccination uptake. Key Details Duration: 3 hours Certificate: Yes Key Skills Developed Behaviour change communication Campaign planning and evaluation Data-driven decision-making Learn More 9. Results-Based Financing in Health This course explores innovative financing models that improve healthcare outcomes. Key Details Duration: 5 weeks Effort: 3–4 hours/week Certificate: Yes What You Will Learn Principles of results-based financing (RBF) Health system strengthening strategies Policy and implementation frameworks Learn More 10. Inner Development Goals for Sustainable Future (Podcast Learning) This learning resource explores the importance of internal human capacities in achieving sustainability. Key Insights Emotional intelligence and leadership Mindfulness and resilience Collaboration and systemic change Role of inner transformation in global development Learn More Why You Should Take These UN Courses These programs provide: Free access to high-quality UN learning resources Globally recognized certificates (for selected courses) Flexible, self-paced learning schedules Practical skills aligned with SDGs Exposure to global case studies and expert insights How to Apply Visit the UN SDG: Learn platform Select your preferred course Register for free Start learning immediately There is no strict deadline for most courses as they are self-paced, but early enrollment is encouraged. Discover more global opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 in San Diego, USA | $1,500 Paid Fellowship for Young Changemakers
summary: Applications are now open for the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 , an exciting paid leadership opportunity for young people in San Diego County who are passionate about advocacy, youth empowerment, and creating change in their communities. The Youth Will LEAD Fellowship—short for Leadership, Empowerment, Advocacy, Development —is a six-week summer fellowship designed to help young leaders gain the knowledge, confidence, and practical skills needed to challenge outdated systems and build better futures for youth. Selected fellows will receive a $1,500 stipend in recognition of their time, expertise, and commitment. About the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 Youth Will believes young people deserve the freedom to pursue careers and life paths that bring joy, fulfillment, and long-term stability. Traditional career pathways often leave young people burdened by debt, stress, and jobs that do not align with their passions. Through this fellowship, participants will explore alternative pathways, accessible education, policy advocacy, and youth wellbeing while helping shape conversations around education and workforce development. Fellowship Benefits Participants in the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 will receive: $1,500 stipend for participation Leadership development opportunities Advocacy and policy engagement experience Political analysis and historical context training Research and survey development skills Networking with like-minded young leaders Real-world experience influencing youth-focused narratives A supportive environment focused on mental health and wellbeing Fellowship Focus Areas Leadership Development Build the confidence and skills to become an effective community advocate and changemaker. Empowerment Through Education Learn about alternative career pathways and accessible opportunities beyond traditional systems. Advocacy Skills Gain hands-on experience in policy discussions, research, and community engagement. Mental Health Wellbeing Promote healthier pathways for youth by challenging pressure-filled and debt-heavy career expectations. Fellowship Timeline The fellowship runs for six weeks during the summer of 2026 . In-Person Session Dates June 13 June 20 June 27 July 11 July 18 July 25 Session Time 10:00 AM – 2:30 PM (In Person) Eligibility Criteria To apply, applicants must: Be 18–25 years old at the time of the fellowship Be based in San Diego County Be available to attend all fellowship session dates Have an interest in leadership, advocacy, youth wellbeing, and community impact Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 is: April 26, 2026 at 11:59 PM Late applications may not be considered, so interested applicants should apply early. How to Apply Applicants can learn more and submit their application through the official website: For questions or additional support, applicants can contact: hello@youthwill.org APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Why You Should Apply If you are a young person ready to make an impact, grow as a leader, and help shape better opportunities for future generations, the Youth Will LEAD Fellowship 2026 is a valuable chance to turn your passion into action while earning financial support. This fellowship is ideal for youth who want to gain real advocacy experience, expand their network, and contribute to meaningful change in their community. title: RCDIJ Africa Fund 2026: Fully Funded Grant for African Journalists (Up to $10,000)
summary: The Ripples Centre for Data and Investigative Journalism Africa Fund is one of the most impactful funding opportunities currently available for journalists across Africa. Designed to strengthen investigative reporting and data-driven storytelling, this grant provides financial and editorial support to journalists working on critical issues affecting the continent. About the RCDIJ Africa Fund The fund is managed by the Ripples Centre for Data and Investigative Journalism (RCDIJ), an independent, non-profit media organization committed to promoting accountability, transparency, and democratic values through investigative journalism. RCDIJ’s mission focuses on: Exposing corruption and systemic failures Strengthening democratic processes Advancing data journalism across Africa Supporting impactful storytelling that influences policy and society Through this fund, RCDIJ empowers journalists to produce high-quality investigative work that addresses pressing challenges across the continent. Funding Details and Benefits The RCDIJ Africa Fund provides both financial and professional support, making it a highly competitive and valuable opportunity. Financial Support Grants range from under $1,000 to $10,000 depending on project scope Funding is determined by: Project scale Geographic coverage Duration of investigation Additional Benefits Editorial mentorship and guidance throughout the project Opportunity for publication on Ripples Nigeria, RCDIJ’s media platform Increased visibility for your investigative work Strengthening of your professional journalism portfolio Thematic Areas Covered The fund supports investigative journalism projects across a wide range of sectors, including: Education Health Security Environment and climate change Democracy and governance Corruption and accountability Economy and finance Migration Technology Culture and development These themes reflect Africa’s most pressing challenges and opportunities for transformation. Who Can Apply? Eligible Category of People This opportunity is specifically designed for: Individual journalists across Africa Eligible applicants include: Employed journalists Freelance journalists Online journalists Print media professionals Broadcast journalists Photojournalists Cartoonists Multimedia journalists Eligible Countries and Regions Continent: Africa Eligible Applicants: Journalists from all African countries This makes the RCDIJ Africa Fund a pan-African opportunity, open to applicants from East, West, Central, North, and Southern Africa. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the RCDIJ Africa Fund, applicants must: Be an individual journalist based in Africa Have a strong interest in investigative or data journalism Demonstrate prior work through published articles or media content Submit a well-structured investigative project proposal Be able to complete the project within the proposed timeline Application Requirements Applicants must submit a single PDF document (in English) containing: Project idea Detailed work plan Budget breakdown Links to previous published works Curriculum Vitae (CV) Data page of an international passport How to Apply Applicants can apply through either of the following methods: Complete the official application form Or send the required PDF document via email Email Submission Details: Email: info@rcdij.org Subject Line: RCDIJ AFRICA FUND APPLICATION Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and applicants are typically notified within about two months after submission. Apply here Why You Should Apply for the RCDIJ Africa Fund The RCDIJ Africa Fund stands out among journalism grants for several reasons: 1. Strong Financial Backing With funding up to $10,000, it enables journalists to undertake ambitious investigative projects. 2. Editorial Support Unlike many grants, this opportunity includes mentorship and editorial guidance. 3. Publication Opportunity Your work may be featured on a recognized platform, increasing reach and credibility. 4. Pan-African Inclusion Open to journalists from all African countries, ensuring diversity and inclusivity. 5. Impact-Driven Focus Projects are designed to influence public discourse and policy. Tips for a Strong Application To increase your chances of success: Choose a compelling, high-impact topic Ensure your proposal is data-driven and evidence-based Present a clear methodology and timeline Provide a realistic and detailed budget Showcase strong previous work samples Focus on originality and investigative depth Deadline Ongoing The RCDIJ Africa Fund is more than just a grant—it is a platform for African journalists to tell powerful stories, uncover hidden truths, and drive meaningful change across the continent. With an ongoing deadline, this opportunity allows you to apply at any time, making it accessible and flexible for journalists at different stages of their careers. title: World Diplomacy Forum Bali 2026: Fully Immersive Leadership Experience with 100+ Scholarships for Global Youth Leaders
summary: The World Diplomacy Forum (WDF) Bali 2026 is a highly immersive, action-driven global leadership experience designed for emerging leaders, diplomats, and changemakers who are ready to test their abilities in real-world scenarios. Taking place in Bali, Indonesia, this unique three-day forum goes far beyond a traditional conference. It places participants directly into high-pressure, real-time simulations involving diplomacy, crisis management, negotiation, and strategic decision-making. Organized by the International School of Leadership and Diplomacy (ISLD), the program is built to develop the next generation of global leaders through experiential learning, global collaboration, and performance-based recognition. Event Date and Location Dates: August 7–9, 2026 Location: Bali, Indonesia Application Process and Timeline Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning early applicants have a higher chance of securing a spot and accessing scholarship opportunities. Participants are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to limited slots and competitive scholarship allocation. What is the World Diplomacy Forum? The World Diplomacy Forum is not a conventional training or lecture-based program. Instead, it is a: Real-time leadership simulation experience High-pressure decision-making environment Interactive diplomacy and negotiation platform Participants are placed in dynamic global scenarios where they must: Lead negotiations Develop policy responses Collaborate across cultures Present strategic solutions under time constraints The program is designed to transform how participants perceive leadership, influence, and global engagement. Core Experience: What Participants Will Do 1. Lead in Real-Time Scenarios Participants are immersed in situations where their leadership decisions directly influence outcomes. 2. Solve a Global AI Governance Crisis Delegates are given 100 minutes to: Research a crisis Develop a policy response Present their solution 3. Compete in “Last Leader Standing” A high-stakes competition testing: Communication Influence Confidence Leadership presence Core Leadership Themes The program is structured around critical leadership competencies, including: Strategic decision-making under pressure AI governance and policy thinking Diplomatic negotiation and influence Crisis communication Leadership presence and personal impact These themes are integrated into all simulations, exercises, and evaluations. Awards and Recognition Participants are assessed continuously throughout the program by professional facilitators. Key Awards Include: Most Influential Delegate The highest individual honor awarded for leadership and influence Master of Crisis Leadership Recognizes the best-performing team during crisis simulations Aspiring Leader Award Honors the most improved participant Diplomatic Impact Award Recognizes excellence in diplomacy and negotiation Grand Privilege Award winners receive: Free access to the Leaders Summit USA 2027 All participants receive: 50% discount on the Leaders Summit USA 2027 Benefits of Attending Participants gain far more than theoretical knowledge. Key Benefits: Practical leadership experience in high-pressure scenarios Global network of peers from 50+ countries Official ISLD Certificate of Participation Exposure to real-world diplomacy simulations Access to exclusive global leadership events Additional Value: Bali cultural experience included in the program Recognition that enhances career and academic profiles Opportunities for long-term collaboration and networking Scholarship Opportunities The forum offers more than 100 scholarships to ensure accessibility for talented individuals worldwide. Scholarship Model: Performance-Based Applicants must complete a leadership assessment quiz after applying. Scholarship Tiers: 90–100: Presidential Scholar Full program fee waiver (with interview) 80–89: Excellence Scholar 50% fee waiver 65–79: Merit Scholar 40% fee waiver 50–64: Access Scholar 30% fee waiver Below 50: No scholarship Important Notes: Scholarships are awarded automatically based on quiz scores No separate scholarship application is required Offers are valid for 72 hours Limited slots per tier Programme Packages Full Experience Package ($849 USD) Includes: Full 3-day program 3 nights accommodation (twin-sharing) Meals and networking events Bali cultural experience Delegate kit and certificate Award eligibility Register Now Programme Only Package ($649 USD) Includes: Full program access Meals and networking sessions Certificate and materials Bali experience No accommodation included Register Now Please note that the fees stated above are before the scholarship. Why You Should Apply The World Diplomacy Forum Bali 2026 offers a rare opportunity to: Experience leadership under real pressure Build global influence and confidence Engage with diverse international peers Gain recognition through performance-based awards Join a global leadership network This is not just a forum—it is a transformational leadership journey. Application Process The application process is structured and competitive: Submit application and pay commitment fee ($15.99) Complete leadership assessment quiz Receive scholarship decision Accept offer within 72 hours Receive official invitation letter Attend mandatory pre-event orientation Prepare and travel to Bali Click HERE to apply For more information about the World Diplomacy Forum (WDF) Bali 2026, visit HERE. Discover more global Conference, Fellowship, Leadership and Scholarship opportunities on OFY. title: UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) 2026 in Vienna: Paid UN Opportunity for Young Professionals from the Global South
summary: The UNIDO Project Associate – Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI) 2026 is a highly competitive early-career opportunity offered by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO). This programme is specifically designed to equip young professionals from underrepresented countries with hands-on experience in international development, industrial policy, and sustainable economic transformation. Hosted at UNIDO Headquarters in Vienna, Austria, this role offers participants the chance to work within a global institution while gaining exposure to real-world projects that advance inclusive and sustainable industrial development. Application Deadline 15 May 2026 (11:59 PM Vienna Time) Applicants must submit their applications through the official UNIDO recruitment platform before the deadline. About UNIDO and the IFTI Programme The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) is a specialized UN agency that promotes: Inclusive and sustainable industrial development Poverty reduction and economic transformation Environmental sustainability and climate action Innovation and industrial competitiveness The Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI) is a 24-month early-career programme developed to: Strengthen UNIDO’s global talent pipeline Increase representation from underrepresented Member States Empower youth and promote gender equality Provide professional experience within international development systems The programme aligns with global priorities such as the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 9 (industry, innovation, and infrastructure). Position Overview Role Title Project Associate – Industrial Futures Talent Initiative (IFTI) Location Vienna, Austria (UNIDO Headquarters) Contract Duration 1 year (renewable for an additional year) Intake Structure Two intake rounds (2026 and 2027) Approximately 6–8 candidates selected in total What the Programme Offers The UNIDO IFTI Programme provides a robust professional development experience combining technical exposure, mentorship, and real-world project work. Key Benefits Competitive Salary Approximately EUR 4,000 gross per month Additional Benefits Air travel allowance (to and from Vienna) 50% medical insurance subsidy Professional Development Exposure to technical, economic, and industrial development Hands-on experience in project design and management Mentorship and Support Assigned mentor and buddy Structured mentoring programme Global Work Environment Work in a multicultural UN setting Collaborate with international experts Career Growth Build expertise in policy, research, and development Strengthen your profile for international careers Programme Components Participants will engage in a comprehensive learning and working experience, including: 1. Technical and Industrial Exposure Gain insights into industrial development trends Work on sustainability, innovation, and economic competitiveness 2. Project Design and Management Participate across the full project lifecycle Learn planning, implementation, and evaluation processes 3. On-the-Job Training Contribute to real UNIDO programmes Develop practical professional skills 4. Mentoring Programme Receive guidance from experienced UN professionals Participate in peer learning and networking Key Responsibilities Selected candidates will support UNIDO programmes and projects by: Conducting research on economic, industrial, and environmental topics Preparing reports, presentations, and analytical documents Supporting project design, monitoring, and evaluation Maintaining documentation and institutional records Assisting in organizing events, trainings, and missions Contributing to policy and technical cooperation activities Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) Programme, applicants must meet the following requirements: 1. Age Requirement Must be between 23 and 28 years old 2. Educational Qualification Bachelor’s degree (minimum requirement) 3. Work Experience At least 2 years of relevant professional experience 4. Technical Background (Desirable) Experience in: Industrial development Sustainability Economic competitiveness Technical cooperation 5. Geographic Eligibility Applicants must be nationals of eligible underrepresented UNIDO Member States (primarily from the Global South). 6. Language Requirement Fluency in English (required) Additional UN language (desirable) 7. Additional Requirements Interest in sustainable industrial development Experience in multicultural environments (advantage) Diversity and Inclusion UNIDO strongly encourages: Women candidates to apply Persons with disabilities to apply Applicants from underrepresented countries The organization is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion in its workforce. Required Competencies Applicants are expected to demonstrate: Core Values Integrity Professionalism Respect for diversity Key Competencies Teamwork and collaboration Results-oriented mindset Effective communication Innovation and problem-solving Why You Should Apply The UNIDO IFTI Programme is one of the most valuable entry points into the United Nations system for young professionals. Key Reasons to Apply Gain international work experience in the UN Receive competitive remuneration and benefits Build a global professional network Develop expertise in industrial development and policy Contribute to global sustainable development goals How to Apply Applicants must: Submit an online application via the UNIDO Recruitment System Ensure all eligibility criteria are met Prepare required documents and information Apply before the deadline For more details about the UNIDO Project Associate (IFTI) 2026 in Vienna, visit HERE. Discover more global Job opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Postdoctoral Fellowship in International Law 2026 – Centre for International Law, NUS (Fully Funded Research Opportunity in Singapore)
summary: The Centre for International Law (CIL) at the National University of Singapore is inviting applications for Postdoctoral Fellowship positions commencing in September 2026. This is a research-focused opportunity designed for early-career scholars aiming to pursue academic careers in international law. About the Centre for International Law (CIL) The Centre for International Law is a university-level research centre dedicated to producing cutting-edge scholarship in international law. It plays a leading role in capacity-building across the Asia-Pacific region through: Academic workshops and conferences Professional training sessions Research-driven programmes engaging academics, government officials, and private sector professionals CIL is also strongly oriented toward supporting academic development and policy-relevant research in international law. Fellowship Overview The Postdoctoral Fellowship is a one-year appointment, with the possibility of renewal for a second year based on satisfactory performance during the first year. Start date: September 2026 Duration: 1 year (renewable for a second year) Location: Bukit Timah Campus, Singapore Employee category: Research Staff Posted on: 19 January 2026 The programme is directed by Dr. Nilufer Oral (Director of CIL and member of the International Law Commission), together with Professor Joseph Weiler, visiting professor at NUS. Research Areas Applications are open to candidates working in all areas of international law. However, special consideration will be given to research projects focused on: The Asia-Pacific region Climate change law ASEAN law and policy Fellows are encouraged to pursue research that is both academically rigorous and policy relevant. Programme Objectives and Academic Environment The fellowship is specifically designed to support scholars pursuing academic careers. Fellows will benefit from: Individual academic mentoring Workshops on research design and academic writing Training in publication strategies and teaching methods Guidance on supervision and methodological development Fellows will also actively participate in the academic life of CIL, including presenting research, contributing to discussions, and engaging with visiting scholars. Qualifications and Selection Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: A PhD in international law obtained within the last two years OR Doctoral candidates who will complete their dissertation by 31 July 2026 Demonstrated research excellence and originality in the doctoral dissertation Strong academic potential and a well-developed postdoctoral research proposal Required Submission Materials: PhD dissertation (PDF) Postdoctoral research proposal One or two samples of published work Letters of Recommendation: Three confidential reference letters are required Ideally, one should be from the PhD supervisor or examiner Referees must send letters directly to: huong.tam@nus.edu.sg References must assess: Dissertation quality Proposed research project Academic potential Application Procedure Applications must be submitted online via the NUS career portal. Deadlines: Preferred deadline: 30 April 2026 Final deadline: 15 May 2026 Required Documents: Personal statement (500–600 words) outlining motivation, career goals, and relevant background Full curriculum vitae (including education, employment history, publications, awards, and honours) PhD dissertation and doctoral certification Research proposal (maximum 3000 words), including: Research questions Methodology Expected contribution to the field Anticipated outputs (e.g., journal articles, book manuscript) Executive summary (maximum 600 words) One or two writing samples Three academic references (submitted separately) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Programme Expectations Selected Fellows are expected to: Carry out the proposed research project during the fellowship period Publish or secure acceptance for at least one or two peer-reviewed articles (or a book proposal with a reputable publisher) Participate actively in workshops and academic discussions Present research at conferences, including at NUS and international venues Be in residence at NUS for the duration of the appointment (with limited approved travel for research and fieldwork) Standard NUS leave policies apply. Additional Information Location: Bukit Timah Campus, Singapore Organization: Centre for International Law, NUS Job Category: Research Staff Reference ID: 31488 Application status: Open until 15 May 2026 Notification: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare all required documents and submit their application through the official NUS career portal before the deadline. Early submission is strongly encouraged due to preference for applications received by 30 April 2026. APPLY HERE Discover more similar opportunities Here.
